pr.title: add global headers for function and variable declarations
pr.author: camthesaxman

summary: Added two large global declaration headers, include/functions.h and include/variables.h, containing thousands of commented-out placeholder function and variable declarations. The PR body states the intended workflow: include functions.h and variables.h in C files, then uncomment the declaration needed, reducing the pain of adding declarations and keeping them consistent across C files.
what_changed: Created include/functions.h with 9122 added lines of commented placeholder function declarations.
Created include/variables.h with 10036 added lines of commented extern placeholder variable declarations.
functions.h groups symbols by executable section at least for .init and .text, with examples such as func_800031F4, TRK_memcpy, __start, HSD_JObjGetMtxPtr, and many func_80xxxxxx symbols.
variables.h starts with commented extern placeholders such as lbl_80005918 and lbl_803B7240-style labels.

The declarations use unknown-type placeholders, e.g. comment lines of the form '// ? func_800031F4(?);' and '// extern ? lbl_803B7240;', so they are available as templates but do not affect compilation until edited and uncommented.
smart_moves: Centralized shared declarations into global headers to avoid every C file growing its own inconsistent local prototypes or externs.
Kept every generated/unknown declaration commented out, allowing contributors to opt in only to declarations they have validated or need for a file.
Used placeholder '?' syntax to preserve symbol names and intended declaration shape while clearly marking unknown return types, argument lists, and variable types.
Organized functions by section comments such as '.init section' and '.text section', making the symbol catalog easier to scan against the map/order.
Separated functions and variables into distinct headers, which makes future promotion from placeholder to real prototype/extern easier to search and maintain.
decomp_lessons: For early-stage decompilation, a central commented symbol catalog can be useful infrastructure: it gives contributors a consistent starting point without forcing unverified declarations into the build.
When a function signature or variable type is unknown, keep the declaration inactive and visibly incomplete rather than committing a guessed active prototype.
Use global declaration headers as a staging area, then uncomment and refine only the specific symbol needed by a translation unit.
Keeping symbol names exactly as known from addresses or libraries, such as func_800031F4, lbl_803B7240, TRK_memcpy, and HSD_JObjGetMtxPtr, supports searchability and cross-file consistency.
Large mechanical header additions can be valuable if their workflow purpose is clear: here the PR body explicitly says the goal was to make adding declarations less painful and more consistent.
naming_conventions: Unknown functions retain address-based names in the form func_800031F4 with uppercase hexadecimal digits.
Unknown data labels retain lbl_XXXXXXXX-style names, e.g. lbl_803B7240.
Known SDK/library-style names are preserved where available, including TRK_memcpy, __check_pad3, __start, and HSD_JObjGetMtxPtr.
Function placeholders are written as commented prototypes with unknown return and parameters: '// ? Symbol(?);'.
Variable placeholders are written as commented externs with unknown type: '// extern ? Symbol;'.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use functions.h and variables.h as a reusable declaration source while matching C files: include the header, then uncomment or refine only the declaration required for the current match.
The commented '?' placeholders prevent accidental compiler-visible incorrect signatures, which is important because wrong prototypes can change codegen and break matching.
Section grouping in functions.h can help locate nearby symbols in map/disassembly order when resolving calls or extern references.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the slice.
One review by RevoSucks approved the PR with an empty body.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that uncomment or refine declarations from include/functions.h or include/variables.h.
Search for later migrations from global headers to subsystem-specific headers.
Look for review discussions in later PRs about replacing '?' placeholders with concrete prototypes or extern types.
Search for include/functions.h usage in C files to see how the intended workflow evolved.
